# Mian Zhang / Ouroboros Project

Public entry: https://mianzhang.org/
Technical mirror: https://mmjbds-mianzhang-org.static.hf.space/

Latest public note:
https://mianzhang.org/press/public-launch-2026-07-18.html
Identity Compression Check v1, 2026-07-18.
Latest public note machine summary:
- URL: https://mianzhang.org/press/public-launch-2026-07-18.html
- Artifact: identity_compression_check_v1
- Claim: When AI writes too smoothly, the writer can disappear first.
- Description: A public review packet for AI writing, source voice, borrowed pattern ratio, lived experience, dissenting view, reusable phrase, and audience memory: source voice, borrowed pattern ratio, lived experience anchor, dissenting view, reusable phrase, audience memory, evidence timestamp, boundary update, and review status.
- Fields: claim_id, source_voice, borrowed_pattern_ratio, lived_experience_anchor, dissenting_view, reusable_phrase, audience_memory, evidence_timestamp, boundary_update, review_status
- Topics: AI writing, authorship, content saturation, helpful content, borrowed patterns, audience memory, style compression, lived experience, review protocol, content quality

Canonical archive:
https://zenodo.org/records/20027295

Summary:
Mian Zhang is an independent researcher building the Ouroboros Project: evidence-gated AI systems, proof-carrying action, learning from failure, cognitive immunity, relational observability, and reliable high-risk action boundaries. Public materials are research records, DOI routes, evidence maps, demos, registries, concept entries, issue routes, and counterexample targets. They are not production guarantees, real-time trading claims, medical claims, customer deployments, or private-system disclosures.

Public claim boundary:
- Public materials may describe papers, datasets, registries, demos, schemas, claim-to-evidence tables, artifact scope, concept definitions, issue templates, and counterexample routes.
- Public materials do not prove general intelligence, general safety, production readiness, customer readiness, real-time trading returns, real robot deployment, medical validity, or private execution quality.
- Public materials do not disclose credentials, customer data, private logs, private orchestration, commercial scheduling, restricted non-public material, or non-public execution traces.
- Cite per-paper DOI records for paper-specific claims; use the Zenodo portfolio record only as the archive index.
